metric_value: SD Memory Cards captured almost 25% of the sector approximately $81M in US retail sales in January and February 2003, overtaking Sony Memory Stick for the first time.
Consumer demand for higher capacity and faster access removable media propelled SD cards to 400 percent sales growth in 2002, per NPD Group.

Consumers are gravitating to SD based on form factor, price, wide availability and extensive interchangeability among consumer digital electronics. The rapid consumer adoption of MP3 players, PDAs, digital cameras and camcorders are driving SD demand.
notes: Kastner as Aberdeen CRO quoted in Computing

metric_value: We think that the miniSD will become a critical technology enabler in next-generation cellphones.
notes: Kastner forecast — miniSD/microSD eventually enabled expansion in feature phones / early smartphones

metric_value: The SD format is supported by 550+ companies through the SD Card Association; consumer demand increased more than fivefold in 2002.
Sony officially discontinued Memory Stick production for cameras in 2013-2014; the format has been fully supplanted globally by SD/microSD.

miniSD was launched March 2003 but largely supplanted by the smaller microSD (TransFlash, 2005) in mobile phones. Kastner miniSD-in-phones prediction validated in spirit though format branding evolved.
